# @functionspace/react -- Agent Implementation Guide

This document is a focused implementation guide for AI coding assistants working with the `@functionspace/react` package. It covers the headless custom UI integration mode -- provider setup, data-fetching hooks, state and action hooks, belief construction, trade execution patterns, previews, and the context API. This mode uses `@functionspace/core` + `@functionspace/react` without pre-built UI widgets, giving full control over rendering.

```
Packages:
  @functionspace/core   -- Pure TypeScript. API client, probability math, belief construction, trade execution. Zero framework dependencies.
  @functionspace/react  -- React integration. Provider, hooks, theme system. Depends on @functionspace/core.

Install (headless React -- hooks only, no pre-built components):
  npm install @functionspace/core @functionspace/react
```

> **Note:** These packages are not yet published to npm. To use the SDK,
> clone the repository and use npm workspace linking. The `npm install`
> commands below are forward-looking and will work once packages are published.

## Table of Contents

- [1. Overview](#1-overview)
- [2. Known Limitations](#2-known-limitations)
- [3. Setup](#3-setup)
- [4. Data Hooks Reference](#4-data-hooks-reference)
- [5. State & Action Hooks](#5-state--action-hooks)
- [6. Building Beliefs](#6-building-beliefs)
- [7. Mutation Hooks (Trade Execution)](#7-mutation-hooks-trade-execution)
- [8. Preview Hooks](#8-preview-hooks)
- [9. Context API Quick Reference](#9-context-api-quick-reference)
- [10. NEVER Rules (Headless)](#10-never-rules-headless)
- [11. Type Reference](#11-type-reference)

---

## 1. Overview

### 1.1 Machine-Readable Preamble

```
SDK: FunctionSpace Trading SDK
Version: 1.x (pre-release)
Packages:
  @functionspace/core   -- Pure TypeScript. API client, probability math, belief construction, trade execution. Zero framework dependencies.
  @functionspace/react  -- React integration. Provider, hooks, theme system. Depends on @functionspace/core.
  @functionspace/ui     -- Pre-built React widgets. Charts, trade panels, position tables. Depends on @functionspace/core and @functionspace/react.

Install (headless React -- hooks only, no pre-built components):
  npm install @functionspace/core @functionspace/react

Description: A TypeScript SDK for embedding prediction market trading widgets into web applications. Three packages form a strict dependency chain: core (pure TS math and API client) -> react (hooks, Provider, theming) -> ui (pre-built interactive components).
```

### 1.2 What This Package Does

The `@functionspace/react` package provides the React integration layer for the FunctionSpace Trading SDK. It includes `FunctionSpaceProvider` (which manages auth, theming, and shared state), data-fetching hooks (`useMarket`, `useConsensus`, `usePositions`, etc.), mutation hooks (`useBuy`, `useSell`), preview hooks (`usePreviewPayout`, `usePreviewSell`), state hooks (`useAuth`, `useCustomShape`, `useChartZoom`), and the context API for cross-component coordination.

### 1.3 Package Dependency Chain

```
@functionspace/core       Pure TypeScript. API client, probability math,
        |                 belief construction, trade execution. No framework
        |                 dependencies. Works in Node.js, browsers, or any
        |                 JavaScript runtime.
        v
@functionspace/react      React integration layer. FunctionSpaceProvider
        |                 manages auth, theming, and shared state. Hooks
        |                 wrap core functions for React consumption.
        v
@functionspace/ui         Pre-built React widgets. ConsensusChart, TradePanel,
                          PositionTable, ShapeCutter, and more. Fully themed
                          and self-contained.
```

This document covers `@functionspace/core` + `@functionspace/react` (headless custom UI mode).

### 1.4 Authentication Modes

| Mode | Config | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| **Auto-auth** | Pass `username` and `password` in the `config` object | The Provider authenticates automatically on mount; no login UI or manual token management required. |
| **Interactive** | Omit `username` and `password` from `config` | Use the `useAuth()` hook to present login/signup forms; the user authenticates at runtime. |
| **Guest** | No credentials anywhere | Read-only access to market data; trading operations (`buy`, `sell`) are blocked server-side. |

---

## 2. Known Limitations

| # | Limitation | Impact | Workaround |
|---|-----------|--------|------------|
| 2 | **No real-time transport (WebSocket/SSE).** The SDK has no push-based data delivery. All data freshness depends on polling or explicit refetch calls. Changes made by other users are not automatically visible without polling. | Market state, consensus curves, and position data go stale between refetches. In multi-user scenarios, the local view drifts from server state until the next poll or refetch. | Use `pollInterval` on any data hook to poll for updates (e.g., `useConsensus(marketId, 200, { pollInterval: 3000 })`). Call `ctx.invalidate(marketId)` after local trades for immediate refetch. |
| 4 | **No market search or filtering.** `discoverMarkets()` returns all available markets as a flat `MarketState[]` array. The function accepts no search, filter, sort, or pagination parameters. | Applications that need to display a subset of markets must fetch the entire market list on every call. There is no server-side filtering. | Call `discoverMarkets(client)` and filter the returned array client-side using standard `Array.filter()`, `Array.sort()`, or `Array.find()` methods. |
| 7 | **Settlement not yet implemented.** The `Position` type includes `settlementPayout` and `MarketState` includes `resolvedOutcome` fields, but no settlement functions exist in the SDK. | Positions in resolved markets cannot be settled through the SDK. The `settlementPayout` field is always `null` in current responses. | Monitor the `resolutionState` field on `MarketState` for `'resolved'` or `'voided'` status. Settlement processing must be handled outside the SDK until settlement functions are added. |

---

## 3. Setup

### Installation

Install the core computation library and the React integration layer. The `@functionspace/ui` package is intentionally excluded -- you are building your own UI.

```bash
npm install @functionspace/core @functionspace/react
```

### Provider Setup

Every headless integration requires a `FunctionSpaceProvider` at the root of the widget tree. The provider creates the API client, manages authentication, resolves theming, and exposes shared state via React context.

```tsx
import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client';
import { FunctionSpaceProvider } from '@functionspace/react';

const config = {
  baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
  username: 'trader1',
  password: 'secret',
  autoAuthenticate: true,
};

function App() {
  return (
    <FunctionSpaceProvider config={config} theme="fs-dark">
      <YourCustomUI />
    </FunctionSpaceProvider>
  );
}

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(<App />);
```

The `config` object accepts these fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `baseUrl` | `string` | Yes | Base URL of the FunctionSpace API server |
| `username` | `string` | No | Username for auto-authentication |
| `password` | `string` | No | Password for auto-authentication |
| `autoAuthenticate` | `boolean` | No | When `true` (the default if `username` and `password` are provided), the provider logs in automatically on mount |

The `FunctionSpaceProvider` component also accepts these optional props:

| Prop | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `config` | `FSConfig` | Yes | Configuration object (see table above) |
| `theme` | `FSThemeInput` | No | Theme preset string or custom theme object |
| `cache` | `CacheConfig` | No | Cache configuration: `{ staleTime?, gcTime?, defaultPollInterval?, revalidateOnFocus?, revalidateOnReconnect? }` |
| `storedUsername` | `string \| null` | No | Previously stored username for silent re-authentication on mount |

### Import Map

Headless integrations pull from two packages. Core functions and types come from `@functionspace/core`. Hooks, the provider, the context object, and theme utilities come from `@functionspace/react`.

```tsx
// --- @functionspace/core ---
// Belief generators
import {
  generateBelief,
  generateGaussian,
  generateRange,
  generateDip,
  generateLeftSkew,
  generateRightSkew,
  generateCustomShape,
  generateBellShape,
} from '@functionspace/core';

// Transactions (state-changing)
import { buy, sell } from '@functionspace/core';

// Previews (read-only previews)
import { previewPayoutCurve, previewSell } from '@functionspace/core';

// Pure math (no network)
import {
  evaluateDensityCurve,
  computeStatistics,
  computePercentiles,
  calculateBucketDistribution,
} from '@functionspace/core';

// Types
import type {
  MarketState,
  MarketConfig,
  BeliefVector,
  Position,
  BuyResult,
  SellResult,
  PayoutCurve,
  PreviewSellResult,
  ConsensusCurve,
  BucketData,
  Region,
  PointRegion,
  RangeRegion,
  SplineRegion,
} from '@functionspace/core';

// --- @functionspace/react ---
// Provider and context
import { FunctionSpaceProvider, FunctionSpaceContext } from '@functionspace/react';
import type { FSContext } from '@functionspace/react';

// Data hooks
import {
  useMarket,
  useConsensus,
  usePositions,
  useTradeHistory,
  useMarketHistory,
  useBucketDistribution,
  useDistributionState,
} from '@functionspace/react';

// State and action hooks
import { useAuth, useCustomShape, useChartZoom, rechartsPlotArea } from '@functionspace/react';
```

### Authentication Modes

**Auto-auth (credentials in config).** The provider calls `loginUser()` on mount. All hooks wait for authentication to complete before fetching data. Best for demos, internal tools, and server-rendered pages where credentials are injected from environment variables.

```tsx
const config = {
  baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
  username: import.meta.env.VITE_FS_USERNAME,
  password: import.meta.env.VITE_FS_PASSWORD,
  autoAuthenticate: true,
};
```

**Interactive auth (login via `useAuth()`).** Omit credentials. The provider starts in guest mode (read-only). Your custom UI calls `login()` or `signup()` from the `useAuth()` hook. Charts and data hooks work immediately in read-only mode; trading functions require authentication.

```tsx
const config = {
  baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
  autoAuthenticate: false,
};
```

**Guest mode (read-only).** Same as interactive, but you never call `login()`. Data hooks work. Trading functions will fail with authentication errors. Useful for public dashboards.

---

## 4. Data Hooks Reference

All data-fetching hooks follow a consistent contract. Each returns an object with a named data property, plus `loading`, `isFetching`, `error`, and `refetch`. All data hooks subscribe to the query cache and automatically refetch when `ctx.invalidate(marketId)` is called. All data hooks accept optional `QueryOptions` (`pollInterval`, `enabled`) for cache-based polling and conditional fetching.

**`loading` vs `isFetching`:** `loading` is `true` only on the first fetch (no cached data). On background refetches (polling, invalidation), `loading` stays `false` and `isFetching` is `true`. This prevents UI flicker during polling and background refetches.

### `useMarket(marketId, options?)`

Fetches the complete market state including configuration, consensus, and metadata.

```typescript
function useMarket(
  marketId: string | number,
  options?: QueryOptions,
): {
  market: MarketState | null;
  loading: boolean;
  isFetching: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | The market identifier |
| `options.pollInterval` | `number?` | Polling interval in ms. Default: `0` (no polling). |
| `options.enabled` | `boolean?` | When `false`, suppresses fetching. Default: `true`. |

**Return shape:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `market` | `MarketState \| null` | Complete market state, or `null` while loading |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `true` only on the first fetch (no cached data) |
| `isFetching` | `boolean` | `true` whenever a request is in flight (first fetch or background refetch) |
| `error` | `Error \| null` | Fetch error, if any |
| `refetch` | `() => Promise<void>` | Trigger a background refetch |

The `MarketState` object contains the market configuration (`market.config` with `numBuckets`, `lowerBound`, `upperBound`, and other protocol parameters), the current consensus vector (`market.consensus`), pool statistics (`market.totalMass`, `market.poolBalance`, `market.totalVolume`), metadata (`market.title`, `market.xAxisUnits`, `market.decimals`), and resolution state (`market.resolutionState`).

**Example:**

```tsx
import React, { useContext } from 'react';
import { FunctionSpaceContext, useMarket } from '@functionspace/react';

function MarketHeader({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const { market, loading, error } = useMarket(marketId);

  if (loading) return <div>Loading market...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
  if (!market) return null;

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>{market.title}</h2>
      <p>Range: {market.config.lowerBound} - {market.config.upperBound} {market.xAxisUnits}</p>
      <p>Total volume: ${market.totalVolume.toFixed(2)}</p>
      <p>Open positions: {market.positionsOpen}</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### `useConsensus(marketId, numPoints?, options?)`

Fetches the consensus probability density as a renderable curve (array of `{x, y}` points).

```typescript
function useConsensus(
  marketId: string | number,
  numPoints?: number,
  options?: QueryOptions,
): {
  consensus: ConsensusCurve | null;
  loading: boolean;
  isFetching: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | -- | The market identifier |
| `numPoints` | `number` | `200` | Number of sample points for the density curve |
| `options.pollInterval` | `number?` | `0` | Polling interval in ms. |
| `options.enabled` | `boolean?` | `true` | When `false`, suppresses fetching. |

**Return shape:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `consensus` | `ConsensusCurve \| null` | Object containing `points: Array<{x: number; y: number}>` and `config: MarketConfig` |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `true` only on the first fetch (no cached data) |
| `isFetching` | `boolean` | `true` whenever a request is in flight |
| `error` | `Error \| null` | Fetch error, if any |
| `refetch` | `() => Promise<void>` | Trigger a background refetch |

**Example:**

```tsx
import React from 'react';
import { useConsensus } from '@functionspace/react';

function ConsensusPeakDisplay({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const { consensus, loading, error } = useConsensus(marketId, 200);

  if (loading) return <div>Loading consensus...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
  if (!consensus) return null;

  const peak = consensus.points.reduce(
    (max, pt) => (pt.y > max.y ? pt : max),
    consensus.points[0],
  );

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Most likely outcome: {peak.x.toFixed(2)} (density: {peak.y.toFixed(4)})</p>
      <p>Curve points: {consensus.points.length}</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### `usePositions(marketId, username?, options?)`

Fetches all positions for a market, with an optional filter by username. Filtering is client-side after the cache -- one API call per market regardless of username.

```typescript
function usePositions(
  marketId: string | number,
  username?: string,
  options?: QueryOptions,
): {
  positions: Position[] | null;
  loading: boolean;
  isFetching: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | -- | The market identifier |
| `username` | `string` | `undefined` | When provided, filters positions to this user only. When omitted, returns all market positions. |
| `options.pollInterval` | `number?` | `0` | Polling interval in ms. |
| `options.enabled` | `boolean?` | `true` | When `false`, suppresses fetching. |

**Example:**

```tsx
import React, { useContext } from 'react';
import { FunctionSpaceContext, usePositions } from '@functionspace/react';

function MyPositions({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const ctx = useContext(FunctionSpaceContext);
  if (!ctx) throw new Error('Must be within FunctionSpaceProvider');

  const { positions, loading, error } = usePositions(
    marketId,
    ctx.user?.username,
  );

  if (loading) return <div>Loading positions...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
  if (!positions || positions.length === 0) return <div>No positions</div>;

  const openPositions = positions.filter(p => p.status === 'open');

  return (
    <ul>
      {openPositions.map(pos => (
        <li key={pos.positionId}>
          Position #{pos.positionId}: ${pos.collateral.toFixed(2)} collateral,{' '}
          {pos.claims.toFixed(4)} claims
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}
```

### `useTradeHistory(marketId, options?)`

Fetches trade history entries with optional cache-based polling.

```typescript
function useTradeHistory(
  marketId: string | number,
  options?: { limit?: number; pollInterval?: number; enabled?: boolean },
): {
  trades: TradeEntry[] | null;
  loading: boolean;
  isFetching: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | -- | The market identifier |
| `options.limit` | `number` | `100` | Maximum number of trade entries to return |
| `options.pollInterval` | `number` | `0` | Polling interval in milliseconds. Visibility-aware and deduplicated. |
| `options.enabled` | `boolean?` | `true` | When `false`, suppresses fetching. |

**Example:**

```tsx
import React from 'react';
import { useTradeHistory } from '@functionspace/react';

function LiveTradesFeed({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const { trades, loading, error } = useTradeHistory(marketId, {
    limit: 20,
    pollInterval: 5000, // refresh every 5 seconds
  });

  if (loading) return <div>Loading trades...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
  if (!trades || trades.length === 0) return <div>No trades yet</div>;

  return (
    <ul>
      {trades.map(trade => (
        <li key={trade.id}>
          {trade.timestamp} -- {trade.username} {trade.side}{' '}
          ${trade.amount.toFixed(2)}
          {trade.prediction !== null && ` @ ${trade.prediction}`}
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}
```

### `useMarketHistory(marketId, options?)`

Fetches historical market snapshots (alpha vector changes over time). Used to build fan charts showing how the consensus evolved.

```typescript
function useMarketHistory(
  marketId: string | number,
  options?: { limit?: number; pollInterval?: number; enabled?: boolean },
): {
  history: MarketHistory | null;
  loading: boolean;
  isFetching: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Example:**

```tsx
import React from 'react';
import { useMarketHistory } from '@functionspace/react';
import { transformHistoryToFanChart } from '@functionspace/core';
import { useMarket } from '@functionspace/react';

function HistoryStats({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const { market } = useMarket(marketId);
  const { history, loading, error } = useMarketHistory(marketId, { limit: 100 });

  if (loading || !market || !history) return <div>Loading history...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;

  const { lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
  const fanData = transformHistoryToFanChart(history.snapshots, lowerBound, upperBound);

  return (
    <div>
      <p>Total snapshots: {history.totalSnapshots}</p>
      <p>Fan chart data points: {fanData.length}</p>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### `useBucketDistribution(marketId, numBuckets?, numPoints?)`

Computes a histogram of probability across equal-width outcome buckets.

```typescript
function useBucketDistribution(
  marketId: string | number,
  numBuckets?: number,
  numPoints?: number,
): {
  buckets: BucketData[] | null;
  loading: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  refetch: () => Promise<void>;
}
```

**Example:**

```tsx
import React from 'react';
import { useBucketDistribution } from '@functionspace/react';

function ProbabilityBuckets({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const { buckets, loading, error } = useBucketDistribution(marketId, 10);

  if (loading) return <div>Loading distribution...</div>;
  if (error) return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>;
  if (!buckets) return null;

  return (
    <div>
      {buckets.map((bucket, i) => (
        <div key={i} style={{ display: 'flex', gap: '1rem', marginBottom: '4px' }}>
          <span style={{ width: '120px' }}>{bucket.range}</span>
          <div
            style={{
              width: `${bucket.percentage}%`,
              maxWidth: '300px',
              height: '20px',
              background: '#3b82f6',
            }}
          />
          <span>{bucket.percentage.toFixed(1)}%</span>
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## 5. State & Action Hooks

State and action hooks differ from data hooks: they manage local UI state or expose context actions rather than fetching server data. They do not follow the `{ named, loading, error, refetch }` pattern.

### `useAuth()`

Provides authentication state and actions from the provider context.

```typescript
function useAuth(): {
  user: UserProfile | null;
  isAuthenticated: boolean;
  loading: boolean;
  error: Error | null;
  login: (username: string, password: string) => Promise<UserProfile>;
  signup: (username: string, password: string, options?: SignupOptions) => Promise<UserProfile>;
  logout: () => void;
  refreshUser: () => Promise<void>;
  passwordlessLogin: (username: string) => Promise<PasswordlessLoginResult>;
  showAdminLogin: boolean;
  pendingAdminUsername: string | null;
  clearAdminLogin: () => void;
}
```

**Return shape:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `user` | `UserProfile \| null` | Current user (with `userId`, `username`, `walletValue`, `role`), or `null` if not authenticated |
| `isAuthenticated` | `boolean` | Whether a user session is active |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `true` while an auth operation (login/signup/auto-auth) is in progress |
| `error` | `Error \| null` | Most recent auth error |
| `login` | `(username, password) => Promise<UserProfile>` | Logs in and returns the user profile. The provider stores the token automatically. |
| `signup` | `(username, password, options?) => Promise<UserProfile>` | Registers a new user, then auto-logs-in. Returns the user profile. |
| `logout` | `() => void` | Clears auth token, user, previews, and triggers invalidation |
| `refreshUser` | `() => Promise<void>` | Re-fetches the user profile (call after trades to update wallet balance) |
| `passwordlessLogin` | `(username) => Promise<PasswordlessLoginResult>` | Passwordless login/auto-signup. Returns `{ action, user, token }`. Throws with `code: PASSWORD_REQUIRED` for password-protected accounts. |

**Example:**

```tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { useAuth } from '@functionspace/react';

function LoginForm() {
  const { user, isAuthenticated, loading, error, login, logout } = useAuth();
  const [username, setUsername] = useState('');
  const [password, setPassword] = useState('');

  if (isAuthenticated && user) {
    return (
      <div>
        <span>Logged in as {user.username} (${user.walletValue.toFixed(2)})</span>
        <button onClick={logout}>Sign Out</button>
      </div>
    );
  }

  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    await login(username, password);
  };

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input value={username} onChange={e => setUsername(e.target.value)} placeholder="Username" />
      <input value={password} onChange={e => setPassword(e.target.value)} type="password" placeholder="Password" />
      <button type="submit" disabled={loading}>
        {loading ? 'Logging in...' : 'Log In'}
      </button>
      {error && <p style={{ color: 'red' }}>{error.message}</p>}
    </form>
  );
}
```

### `useCustomShape(market)`

State management for custom shape editing. Manages an array of control point values, locked points, drag state, and automatically computes the resulting belief vector and prediction via `generateCustomShape` and `computeStatistics`.

```typescript
function useCustomShape(market: MarketState | null): UseCustomShapeReturn
```

**Return shape (`UseCustomShapeReturn`):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `controlValues` | `number[]` | Current control point Y-values (0-25 range) |
| `lockedPoints` | `number[]` | Indices of locked control points (max 2) |
| `numPoints` | `number` | Current number of control points (5-25, default 20) |
| `pVector` | `BeliefVector \| null` | The belief vector generated from current control values, or `null` if no market |
| `prediction` | `number \| null` | The mode (most likely outcome) of the belief distribution |
| `setControlValue` | `(index: number, value: number) => void` | Set a specific control point's value (respects locks) |
| `toggleLock` | `(index: number) => void` | Toggle lock state for a control point |
| `setNumPoints` | `(n: number) => void` | Change the number of control points (resets to bell shape) |
| `resetToDefault` | `() => void` | Reset all control values to the default bell shape |
| `startDrag` | `(index: number) => void` | Begin dragging a control point |
| `handleDrag` | `(value: number) => void` | Update the currently dragged point's value |
| `endDrag` | `() => void` | End the drag operation |
| `isDragging` | `boolean` | Whether a drag is currently in progress |
| `draggingIndex` | `number \| null` | Index of the point currently being dragged |

### `useChartZoom(options)`

Scroll-wheel zoom and drag-to-pan state machine for chart containers.

```typescript
function useChartZoom(options: ChartZoomOptions): ChartZoomResult
```

**Parameters (`ChartZoomOptions`):**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `data` | `any[]` | Yes | The data array being charted |
| `xKey` | `string` | Yes | Property name for the x-axis value in each data item |
| `fullXDomain` | `[number, number]` | Yes | The full x-axis range `[min, max]`. Must be memoized -- the hook resets zoom when this value changes. |
| `getPlotArea` | `(rect: DOMRect) => {left, right}` | Yes | Function that returns the pixel boundaries of the plot area. Use the `rechartsPlotArea` helper for Recharts charts. |
| `computeYDomain` | `(visibleData: any[], fullData: any[]) => [number, number]` | No | Custom Y-domain computation. |
| `enabled` | `boolean` | No | Whether zoom/pan is active (default `true`) |

**Return shape (`ChartZoomResult`):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `containerRef` | `React.MutableRefObject<HTMLDivElement \| null>` | Mutable ref to attach to the chart container element |
| `xDomain` | `[number, number]` | Current x-axis domain (changes as user zooms/pans) |
| `yDomain` | `[number, number] \| undefined` | Current y-axis domain (if `computeYDomain` is provided) |
| `isZoomed` | `boolean` | Whether the view is currently zoomed in |
| `isPanning` | `boolean` | Whether a pan drag is in progress |
| `containerProps` | `object` | Event handlers to spread onto the container |
| `reset` | `() => void` | Programmatically reset to the full domain |

The `rechartsPlotArea` helper creates the `getPlotArea` function for Recharts integration:

```typescript
import { rechartsPlotArea } from '@functionspace/react';

const getPlotArea = rechartsPlotArea(
  { left: 20, right: 20 }, // chart margin
  60,                       // y-axis width (optional, default varies)
);
```

### `useDistributionState(marketId, config?)`

Comprehensive distribution state management for bucket-based components. Combines market data, bucket distribution, and percentile computation into a single shareable state object.

```typescript
function useDistributionState(
  marketId: string | number,
  config?: DistributionStateConfig,
): DistributionState
```

**Return shape (`DistributionState`):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `market` | `MarketState \| null` | The market state |
| `loading` | `boolean` | Loading state |
| `error` | `Error \| null` | Error state |
| `refetch` | `() => Promise<void>` | Refetch data |
| `bucketCount` | `number` | Current bucket count |
| `setBucketCount` | `(n: number) => void` | Update bucket count |
| `buckets` | `BucketData[] \| null` | Computed bucket distribution |
| `percentiles` | `PercentileSet \| null` | Computed percentiles (p2.5 through p97.5) |
| `getBucketsForRange` | `(low: number, high: number) => BucketData[]` | Get buckets within a specific range |

---

## 6. Building Beliefs

A belief vector is a `number[]` of length `numBuckets + 1` that sums to 1. It expresses a trader's probability distribution over possible outcomes. Every trade in the SDK starts with constructing a belief.

The three parameters `numBuckets`, `lowerBound`, and `upperBound` always come from the market configuration. Never hardcode them.

```typescript
const { market } = useMarket(marketId);
const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
```

### `generateBelief(regions, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` -- Universal Constructor

The L1 universal constructor. Every other generator delegates to this function.

```typescript
import { generateBelief } from '@functionspace/core';
import type { Region, BeliefVector } from '@functionspace/core';

function generateBelief(
  regions: Region[],
  numBuckets: number,
  lowerBound: number,
  upperBound: number,
): BeliefVector  // number[] of length numBuckets+1, sums to 1
```

### Region Types

**`PointRegion`** -- Gaussian peak (or inverted dip):

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'point'` | -- | Discriminator |
| `center` | `number` | -- | Peak location in outcome space |
| `spread` | `number` | -- | Width of the bell curve in outcome-space units |
| `weight` | `number` | `1` | Relative weight when combining with other regions |
| `skew` | `number` | `0` | Asymmetry. Negative = wider left tail, positive = wider right tail. |
| `inverted` | `boolean` | `false` | When `true`, creates a dip: high at edges, low at center |

**`RangeRegion`** -- Flat-topped range:

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'range'` | -- | Discriminator |
| `low` | `number` | -- | Start of the range in outcome space |
| `high` | `number` | -- | End of the range in outcome space |
| `weight` | `number` | `1` | Relative weight |
| `sharpness` | `number` | `0` | Edge transition: `0` = smooth cosine taper, `1` = hard cliff |

**`SplineRegion`** -- Arbitrary freeform curve:

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `type` | `'spline'` | -- | Discriminator |
| `controlX` | `number[]` | -- | X positions in outcome space `[lowerBound..upperBound]`, sorted ascending |
| `controlY` | `number[]` | -- | Y values (unnormalized heights) |
| `weight` | `number` | `1` | Relative weight |

### Convenience Generators

| Function | Signature | Equivalent `generateBelief` Call |
|----------|-----------|----------------------------------|
| `generateGaussian` | `(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'point', center, spread }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |
| `generateRange` | `(low, high, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound, sharpness?)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'range', low, high, sharpness: sharpness ?? 1 }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |
| `generateDip` | `(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'point', center, spread: spread * 1.5, inverted: true }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |
| `generateLeftSkew` | `(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound, skewAmount?)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'point', center, spread, skew: -skewAmount }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |
| `generateRightSkew` | `(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound, skewAmount?)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'point', center, spread, skew: skewAmount }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |
| `generateCustomShape` | `(controlValues, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` | `generateBelief([{ type: 'spline', controlX: [evenly spaced lowerBound..upperBound], controlY: controlValues }], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound)` |

### Examples

**Single Gaussian -- "I think the outcome will be around 75":**

```typescript
import { generateGaussian } from '@functionspace/core';

const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
const belief = generateGaussian(75, 5, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound);
```

**Bimodal -- "I think it will be either 60 or 90, leaning toward 90":**

```typescript
import { generateBelief } from '@functionspace/core';

const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
const belief = generateBelief([
  { type: 'point', center: 60, spread: 4, weight: 0.3 },
  { type: 'point', center: 90, spread: 4, weight: 0.7 },
], numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound);
```

**Range -- "Somewhere between 70 and 85":**

```typescript
import { generateRange } from '@functionspace/core';

const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
const belief = generateRange(70, 85, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound, 0.5);
```

---

## 7. Mutation Hooks (Trade Execution)

Mutation hooks wrap core trade functions with managed state and automatic cache invalidation. They return `{ execute, loading, error, reset }` -- distinct from data-fetching hooks which return `{ <named>, loading, isFetching, error, refetch }`.

### `useBuy(marketId)`

Opens a new position. Auto-invalidates the market's cache on success.

```typescript
import { useBuy } from '@functionspace/react';

const { execute, loading, error, reset } = useBuy(marketId);
const result = await execute(belief, collateral);
// Cache invalidation happens automatically -- no need to call ctx.invalidate()
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | Market to trade on |

**`execute(belief, collateral)`** -- Submits the trade. Returns `Promise<BuyResult>`. Throws on failure.
**`loading`** -- `true` while the request is in flight.
**`error`** -- Most recent error, or `null`.
**`reset()`** -- Clears `error` to `null`.

The hook reads `numBuckets` from the market cache automatically. If market data is not yet loaded, it throws.

### `useSell(marketId)`

Closes an open position. Auto-invalidates the market's cache on success.

```typescript
import { useSell } from '@functionspace/react';

const { execute, loading, error, reset } = useSell(marketId);
const result = await execute(positionId);
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | Market the position belongs to |

**`execute(positionId)`** -- Closes the position. Returns `Promise<SellResult>`. Throws on failure.

### The Three-Phase Pattern

The SDK's pre-built trading widgets implement a three-phase pattern. When building custom UI, replicate this pattern using mutation hooks.

**Phase 1: Build belief and set preview (instant, every input change).**

```typescript
const belief = generateGaussian(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound);
ctx.setPreviewBelief(belief);
```

**Phase 2: Preview payout (debounced, 500ms after last input change).**

```typescript
const { execute: preview } = usePreviewPayout(marketId);

// Inside a debounced effect:
const curve = await preview(belief, collateral);
ctx.setPreviewPayout(curve);
```

**Phase 3: Submit trade (on user confirmation).**

```typescript
const { execute: executeBuy } = useBuy(marketId);

const result = await executeBuy(belief, collateral);
ctx.setPreviewBelief(null);
ctx.setPreviewPayout(null);
// No need to call ctx.invalidate() -- useBuy does it automatically
```

### Complete Working Example: Custom Trade Form

```tsx
import React, { useState, useContext, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
import { FunctionSpaceContext, useMarket, useBuy, usePreviewPayout } from '@functionspace/react';
import { generateGaussian } from '@functionspace/core';
import type { PayoutCurve } from '@functionspace/core';

function CustomTradeForm({ marketId }: { marketId: string }) {
  const ctx = useContext(FunctionSpaceContext);
  if (!ctx) throw new Error('Must be within FunctionSpaceProvider');

  const { market, loading: marketLoading } = useMarket(marketId);
  const { execute: executeBuy, loading: buyLoading, error: buyError } = useBuy(marketId);
  const { execute: previewPayout } = usePreviewPayout(marketId);

  // User inputs
  const [center, setCenter] = useState(75);
  const [spread, setSpread] = useState(5);
  const [collateral, setCollateral] = useState(10);

  // Local state
  const [payout, setPayout] = useState<PayoutCurve | null>(null);
  const [lastResult, setLastResult] = useState<string | null>(null);

  // Debounce timer ref
  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);

  // Phase 1: Build belief and set preview (instant)
  // Phase 2: Preview payout (debounced 500ms)
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!market) return;
    const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
    const belief = generateGaussian(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound);

    // Phase 1: instant preview
    ctx.setPreviewBelief(belief);

    // Phase 2: debounced payout preview
    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {
      try {
        const curve = await previewPayout(belief, collateral);
        setPayout(curve);
        ctx.setPreviewPayout(curve);
      } catch (err) {
        if (err instanceof DOMException && err.name === 'AbortError') return;
        setPayout(null);
        ctx.setPreviewPayout(null);
      }
    }, 500);

    return () => {
      if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);
    };
  }, [market, center, spread, collateral, marketId]);

  // Clean up previews on unmount
  useEffect(() => {
    return () => {
      ctx.setPreviewBelief(null);
      ctx.setPreviewPayout(null);
    };
  }, []);

  // Phase 3: Submit trade
  const handleBuy = useCallback(async () => {
    if (!market) return;

    try {
      const { numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;
      const belief = generateGaussian(center, spread, numBuckets, lowerBound, upperBound);

      const result = await executeBuy(belief, collateral);
      // Cache invalidation and wallet refresh happen automatically

      ctx.setPreviewBelief(null);
      ctx.setPreviewPayout(null);
      setPayout(null);

      setLastResult(
        `Position #${result.positionId} opened: ${result.claims.toFixed(4)} claims for $${result.collateral}`,
      );
    } catch (err) {
      // error is also available via buyError
    }
  }, [market, center, spread, collateral, marketId]);

  if (marketLoading || !market) return <div>Loading market...</div>;

  const { lowerBound, upperBound } = market.config;

  return (
    <div>
      <h3>Trade: {market.title}</h3>
      <label>
        Prediction ({market.xAxisUnits}):
        <input type="range" min={lowerBound} max={upperBound} step={0.1} value={center}
          onChange={e => setCenter(Number(e.target.value))} />
        <span>{center}</span>
      </label>
      <label>
        Confidence (spread):
        <input type="range" min={1} max={20} step={0.5} value={spread}
          onChange={e => setSpread(Number(e.target.value))} />
        <span>{spread}</span>
      </label>
      <label>
        Collateral ($):
        <input type="number" min={1} value={collateral}
          onChange={e => setCollateral(Number(e.target.value))} />
      </label>
      {payout && (
        <p>Max payout: ${payout.maxPayout.toFixed(2)} at outcome {payout.maxPayoutOutcome.toFixed(1)}</p>
      )}
      <button onClick={handleBuy} disabled={buyLoading || !ctx.isAuthenticated}>
        {buyLoading ? 'Submitting...' : `Buy for $${collateral}`}
      </button>
      {!ctx.isAuthenticated && <p>Log in to trade</p>}
      {buyError && <p style={{ color: 'red' }}>{buyError.message}</p>}
      {lastResult && <p style={{ color: 'green' }}>{lastResult}</p>}
    </div>
  );
}

export default CustomTradeForm;
```

---

## 8. Preview Hooks

Preview hooks wrap read-only core preview functions with managed state and automatic request cancellation. They return the same `{ execute, loading, error, reset }` shape as mutation hooks but do not auto-invalidate the cache.

### `usePreviewPayout(marketId)`

Previews payout curve for a hypothetical trade. Manages its own `AbortController` -- each call to `execute` cancels any previous in-flight request.

```typescript
import { usePreviewPayout } from '@functionspace/react';

const { execute: preview, loading, error } = usePreviewPayout(marketId);
const curve = await preview(belief, collateral);
ctx.setPreviewPayout(curve);
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | Market to preview |

**`execute(belief, collateral)`** -- Returns `Promise<PayoutCurve>`. Automatically reads `numBuckets` from the market cache. Aborts any previous in-flight request.

**`PayoutCurve` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `previews` | `Array<{outcome, payout, profitLoss: number}>` | Array of outcome/payout pairs |
| `maxPayout` | `number` | Best-case payout across all outcomes |
| `maxPayoutOutcome` | `number` | The outcome value that produces the best payout |
| `inputCollateral` | `number` | Echo of the collateral you passed in |

Debouncing is the caller's responsibility (typically 500ms). The hook handles only the request and cancellation.

### `usePreviewSell(marketId)`

Previews how much collateral would be returned if a position were sold. Callers can optionally pass an `AbortSignal` for cancellation.

```typescript
import { usePreviewSell } from '@functionspace/react';

const { execute: previewSellFn, loading, error } = usePreviewSell(marketId);
const result = await previewSellFn(positionId, { signal: controller.signal });
// result = { collateralReturned: number, positionId: string | number }
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `marketId` | `string \| number` | Market the position belongs to |

**`execute(positionId, options?)`** -- Returns `Promise<PreviewSellResult>`. Pass `{ signal }` to enable caller-controlled cancellation.

### Direct Core Imports (Alternative)

The core package also exports `previewPayoutCurve` and `previewSell` for use outside React or when you need full parameter control:

```typescript
import { previewPayoutCurve, previewSell } from '@functionspace/core';

const curve = await previewPayoutCurve(ctx.client, marketId, belief, collateral, numBuckets);
const sellPreview = await previewSell(ctx.client, positionId, marketId);
```

---

## 9. Context API Quick Reference

The context is accessed via `useContext(FunctionSpaceContext)` from `@functionspace/react`. It is `null` outside the provider -- always check before using.

```typescript
import { useContext } from 'react';
import { FunctionSpaceContext } from '@functionspace/react';
import type { FSContext } from '@functionspace/react';

const ctx = useContext(FunctionSpaceContext);
if (!ctx) throw new Error('Must be within FunctionSpaceProvider');
```

### Read Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `client` | `FSClient` | The HTTP client instance (authenticated if provider has credentials) |
| `previewBelief` | `number[] \| null` | Active belief vector preview (set by trade inputs, read by charts) |
| `previewPayout` | `PayoutCurve \| null` | Active payout curve preview |
| `selectedPosition` | `Position \| null` | Currently selected position for chart/table sync |
| `user` | `UserProfile \| null` | Current authenticated user, `null` in guest mode |
| `isAuthenticated` | `boolean` | Whether a user is currently logged in |
| `authLoading` | `boolean` | Whether an auth operation is in progress |
| `authError` | `Error \| null` | Most recent auth error |
| `chartColors` | `ChartColors` | Resolved chart colors for the current theme (concrete hex values for Recharts SVG) |

### Write Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `setPreviewBelief` | `(belief: number[] \| null) => void` | Set/clear the preview belief overlay |
| `setPreviewPayout` | `(payout: PayoutCurve \| null) => void` | Set/clear the payout curve overlay |
| `setSelectedPosition` | `(pos: Position \| null) => void` | Set the selected position |
| `invalidate` | `(marketId: string \| number) => void` | Targeted cache invalidation -- marks cache entries for the given market as stale |
| `invalidateAll` | `() => void` | Global cache invalidation -- marks all cache entries as stale. Called automatically by login, signup, and logout. |
| `login` | `(username: string, password: string) => Promise<UserProfile>` | Interactive login (auto-invalidates all cache entries) |
| `signup` | `(username: string, password: string, options?: SignupOptions) => Promise<UserProfile>` | Interactive signup (auto-logs-in after, auto-invalidates all cache entries) |
| `logout` | `() => void` | Clear auth state, previews, and invalidate all cache entries |
| `refreshUser` | `() => Promise<void>` | Refresh user profile (wallet balance update after trades) |

Total: 18 fields (9 read, 9 write). Additional passwordless auth fields (`passwordlessLogin`, `showAdminLogin`, `pendingAdminUsername`, `clearAdminLogin`) are accessible via `useAuth()`.

---

## 10. NEVER Rules (Headless)

These rules prevent the most common integration mistakes. Each maps to a silent failure or hard-to-debug behavior.

- **NEVER call `buy()` or `sell()` from core directly in React components.** Use `useBuy` and `useSell` mutation hooks instead. They manage loading/error state and auto-invalidate the cache on success. If you must use the core functions directly (e.g., outside React), always call `ctx.invalidate(marketId)` after success.

- **NEVER assume `loading` means a request is in flight (on data-fetching hooks).** `loading` is `true` only on the first fetch (no cached data). For subsequent refetches (polling, invalidation), use `isFetching` to detect in-flight requests. This prevents UI flicker during background refetches. Note: mutation hooks (`useBuy`, `useSell`, etc.) use `loading` differently -- it is `true` for every in-flight request.

- **NEVER create `FSClient` manually inside a React tree.** The provider creates and manages the client instance. Access it via `useContext(FunctionSpaceContext).client`. Creating a second client bypasses auth state, token management, and invalidation coordination.

- **NEVER skip the debounced payout preview step.** The three-phase pattern exists for a reason. Without Phase 2, any chart rendering payout information in its tooltip will show stale or null data. Use `usePreviewPayout` with a 500ms debounce to avoid flooding the API.

- **NEVER use `useFunctionSpace()`.** This hook does not exist. The correct pattern is `useContext(FunctionSpaceContext)` from React, with `FunctionSpaceContext` imported from `@functionspace/react`.

---

## 11. Type Reference

### Core Types (from `@functionspace/core`)

See the complete core type reference in `core.txt`. Key types used in headless mode:

- **`MarketState`** -- Returned by `useMarket()`. Contains `config` (with `numBuckets`, `lowerBound`, `upperBound`), `consensus`, `title`, `xAxisUnits`, `resolutionState`, and pool statistics.
- **`ConsensusCurve`** -- Returned by `useConsensus()`. Contains `points: Array<{x, y}>` and `config`.
- **`Position`** -- Returned by `usePositions()`. Contains `positionId`, `belief`, `collateral`, `claims`, `status`, `prediction`.
- **`BuyResult`** -- Returned by `buy()`. Contains `positionId`, `belief`, `claims`, `collateral`, `positionType?`, `positionParams?`.
- **`SellResult`** -- Returned by `sell()`. Contains `positionId`, `collateralReturned`, `creditedTo?`.
- **`PayoutCurve`** -- Returned by `previewPayoutCurve()`. Contains `previews`, `maxPayout`, `maxPayoutOutcome`.
- **`BeliefVector`** -- `number[]` of length `numBuckets + 1` summing to 1. Passed to `buy()` and `ctx.setPreviewBelief()`. Can be validated with `validateBeliefVector(vector, numBuckets)`.
- **`BucketData`** -- Returned by `useBucketDistribution()`. Contains `range`, `min`, `max`, `probability`, `percentage`.
- **`TradeEntry`** -- Returned by `useTradeHistory()`. Contains `id`, `timestamp`, `side`, `prediction`, `amount`, `username`.

### React Types (from `@functionspace/react`)

- **`FSContext`** -- The shape of the React context object. 19 fields (9 read, 10 write). Accessed via `useContext(FunctionSpaceContext)`.
- **`FSThemeInput`** -- Accepted by the `theme` prop on `FunctionSpaceProvider`. Can be a preset string (`"fs-dark"`, `"fs-light"`, `"native-dark"`, `"native-light"`) or an object with overrides.
- **`CacheConfig`** -- Accepted by the `cache` prop on `FunctionSpaceProvider`. Configures `staleTime`, `gcTime`, `defaultPollInterval`, `revalidateOnFocus`, `revalidateOnReconnect`.
- **`QueryOptions`** -- Accepted by all data-fetching hooks. Configures `pollInterval`, `enabled`.
- **`ChartColors`** -- Concrete hex values for Recharts SVG. Accessible via `ctx.chartColors`. Includes `grid`, `axisText`, `consensus`, `previewLine`, `payout`, `positions`, `fanBands`.

### Theme Presets

```typescript
type ThemePresetId = 'fs-dark' | 'fs-light' | 'native-dark' | 'native-light';
```

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `'fs-dark'` | FunctionSpace branded dark theme (vibrant purples, blues) |
| `'fs-light'` | FunctionSpace branded light theme |
| `'native-dark'` | Neutral dark theme (minimal branding, suitable for embedding) |
| `'native-light'` | Neutral light theme |

---

*Generated for AI coding assistants. Source: FunctionSpace Trading SDK `@functionspace/react` package. For the complete multi-package guide, see `llms.txt`.*
